steps.title: Identify the marked angle at line k
steps.content: The diagram shows one angle at the intersection of transversal $l$ and line $k$ labeled as $40°$. This angle is positioned above line $k$ on the right side of the transversal — an interior angle. Since vertical angles are equal, the angle above line $k$ on the left side of the transversal is also $40°$.

steps.title: Recognize angle relationships with parallel lines
steps.content: When a transversal crosses two lines, the lines are parallel if and only if corresponding angles are equal. Corresponding angles are in the same position at each intersection. The angle $w°$ (above line $j$, right side of the transversal) and the $40°$ angle (above line $k$, right side of the transversal) are in corresponding positions.
steps.formula: $$w° \text{ at line } j \text{ corresponds to } 40° \text{ at line } k$$

steps.title: Apply the corresponding angles test
steps.content: For lines $j$ and $k$ to be parallel, the corresponding angles must be equal. If $w = 40$, then the angle above line $j$ on the right equals the angle above line $k$ on the right. This is precisely the condition for parallel lines. That matches choice A — A is correct.
